threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Columbia Road Flower Market' instead of just 'the market'—it's like you're asking for a direction back to the suburbs.
Heard a group on the N37 arguing over who lost a shoe. Turned out it was all for a bet on some match. Night buses have the best stories.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a mismatched café opening at dawn, with a dog casually perched on the counter, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, I saw a café where the barista served coffee in vintage porcelain from thrift shops. Each cup tells a story—such a quirky touch!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their ability to navigate the dodgy construction zones while still dodging the taco trucks at lunchtime.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a woman give her seat to an elderly man on the 14 Mission while the driver serenaded us with old R&B hits. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into any bar and find tapas, but locals know to avoid the tourist traps around Plaça del Sol. Hidden gems are often just streets away.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still don’t know about the €1.50 menu del día at the small, tucked-away spots; they flock to the tourist traps instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look stunned when the bus driver barely slows down for the stop. It’s like they think we’ll just teleport out.
Caught the 59 last night, and a guy tried to sell a half-eaten kebab. 'Gourmet leftovers!' he called it. Only in Brixton, I swear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old tram seat as a swing in the park—only in Neukölln do you find that mix of creativity and chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab place open at 3 AM blasting techno while serving the best late-night döner. It's a vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know when the burrito joint has run out of guac before even stepping inside. Tourists don’t check the specials and end up ordering the wrong thing.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo ride the 14 Mission during rush hour, completely unfazed by the chaos. Only in San Francisco...

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think they can just pop into a bar and find sangria, but most locals here prefer vermut on a Sunday afternoon. Totally different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle to order a simple 'café con leche' without an accent. Always asking for 'milk coffee' instead.
